meaning: they believed (referring to those addressed in يٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ)
and you all protect yourselves! (command to a group)
indeed (emphasizes that اللَّهَ is سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ)
O you all who (addressing those who believe)
to some (related to كَجَهْرِ - like the loud voice of)
that it fails/becomes worthless (result of لَا تَرْفَعُوٓا۟)
you (all) perceive (in لَا تَشْعُرُونَ - while you do not perceive)
Those who - connects descriptions to people
they lower/reduce (their voices before رَسُولِ ٱللَّهِ)
their voices (object of يَغُضُّونَ - they lower)
those who (subject of يَغُضُّونَ - they lower)
for them (referring to those who lower their voices before the Messenger of Allah)
great/mighty (describing the reward mentioned in أَجْرٌ)
those who (plural - referring to the people calling out)
most of them (subject of لَا يَعْقِلُونَ)
until (connects to تَخْرُجَ - you come out)
would have been (part of conditional statement with لَوْ)
and Allah (as the subject of غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ)
they believed/trusted (subject of الَّذِينَ - those who)
if (introduces a condition for جَآءَكُمْ - 'comes to you')
a rule-breaker/disobedient person (describes someone who brings نَبَإٍ/news)
with a news (connected to جَآءَكُمْ - came to you)
you (all) strike/affect (in context: lest you strike/harm)
a people (object of تُصِيبُوا - that you might strike/afflict)
in ignorance (describing how something was done)
so you (all) become/enter morning (result of تَبَيَّنُوا)
what (refers to فَعَلْتُمْ - what you did)
if (hypothetical - something that didn't actually happen)
he obeys you all (referring to the companions of the Prophet ﷺ)
in (connects قُلُوبِكُمْ 'your hearts' to زَيَّنَهُ 'He made it attractive')
and but indeed (connects to previous statement)
Allah (as the object of حَبَّبَ - made beloved)
to you all (object of وَكَرَّهَ - and He made hateful)
and he made it very beautiful
and He made (them) hate (connected to حَبَّبَ in previous ayah)
to you all (connected to حَبَّبَ - made beloved)
the rejection (of truth) (object of كَرَّهَ - He made hateful)
and the refusing to follow orders (as object)
All-Wise (as a general attribute of اللَّهِ)
between them both (referring to the two groups of believers)
then if (connects to فَأَصْلِحُوا۟ - then make peace)
one of them both (referring to the two groups mentioned)
the one which/that (feminine) (referring to the group that transgresses)
she acts wrongfully (subject of the verb in this ayah)
Allah (as the object of يُحِبُّ - He loves)
the ones who act fairly (object of يُحِبُّ)
the believers (brothers in faith with other believers)
so you all fix/make right! (command to fix relations between أَخَوَيْكُمْ)
and all of you protect yourselves! (command to group)
so that you may/perhaps you will (subject of تُرْحَمُونَ)
they believed (referring to the believers being addressed)
a group (subject of يَسْخَرْ - let not mock)
a people/folk (being acted upon by مِّن)
they are/will be (referring to a group of men who may be better than those mocking them)
better than them (as object of يَكُنَّ)
they mocked each other with nicknames
the breaking away from obedience (subject of بِئْسَ)
and not (connects to previous statement while negating what follows)
and not (connects to previous command ٱجْتَنِبُوا۟ - avoid)
he backbites (in negative command with لا)
does he love? (introducing a rhetorical question about أَحَدُكُمْ)
that (connects to يَأْكُلَ - he would eat)
and so you all hated it
Allah (as the object of وَٱتَّقُوا۟ - fear/be mindful of)
indeed/certainly (emphasizes that اللَّهَ is تَوَّابٌ رَّحِيمٌ)
Allah (as the object of وَٱتَّقُوا۟ - fear/be mindful of)
O you all! (calling attention to a group)
the people (being addressed in this verse)

from (connected to خَلَقْنٰكُم - We created you)
a male (possessed by/belonging to something mentioned in مِّن before it)

peoples, nations (object of جَعَلْنٰكُمْ - we made you)
so that you may get to know each other

the most pious of you all (object of إِنَّ)
Allah (as the object being referred to)
All-Knowing (as one of Allah's perfect attributes)
the bedouins (subject of قَالَتِ - they said)
say! (Allah commands Muhammad ﷺ to say something to the Bedouins)
and not yet (in relation to يَدْخُلِ - has entered)
the faith (that hasn't entered قُلُوبِكُمْ/your hearts)
Allah (as the object of تُطِيعُوا۟ - you all obey)
he decreases from you all (object of لا in this ayah)
most forgiving (as a quality of اللَّهَ)
indeed only/verily only (introduces the true believers in ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ)
those who (referring to believers mentioned before)
and His messenger (referring to Allah's messenger)
of Allah (object of belief in ءَامَنُوا۟ بِ)
those (pointing to the true believers mentioned before)

Allah (as the object being taught about)
about your way of life (in response to أَتُعَلِّمُونَ)
and Allah (as the subject of يَعْلَمُ - knows)
he knows (what is in the heavens and earth)
and Allah (as the subject of يَعْلَمُ)

you (all) act/work/do (in response to what اللَّهُ sees)
